content:
Armin Lederer is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering at the National University of Singapore (NUS), where he was awarded a Presidential Young Professorship. His research focuses on ensuring the safety of unknown systems through a combination of model-based control and supervised machine learning.
Before joining NUS in May 2025, he was a postdoctoral researcher at ETH Zurich in the Learning & Adaptive Systems group and an associated researcher in the ETH AI Center. He received his PhD, Master’s, and Bachelor’s degrees from the Technical University of Munich.
summary: Armin Lederer is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering at the National University of Singapore (NUS), where he was awarded a Presidential Young Professorship. His research focuses on ensuring the safety of unknown systems through a combination of model-based control and supervised machine learning.
title: Armin Lederer

content:
Lei Zheng is a Research Fellow with Prof. Armin Lederer in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering at NUS. His research bridges theory and practice to create safe, reliable, and trustworthy autonomous systems, with a focus on robot safety, motion planning, and safe learning-based control.
He obtained his Ph.D. in Robotics and Autonomous Systems from HKUST (2025). He was a Visiting Ph.D. Student at the Robotics Institute, School of Computer Science, Carnegie Mellon University, Pittsburgh, United States (2025). He previously worked as a Senior Robotics Engineer at XAG.
summary: Lei Zheng is a Research Fellow with Prof. Armin Lederer in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering at NUS. His research bridges theory and practice to create safe, reliable, and trustworthy autonomous systems, with a focus on robot safety, motion planning, and safe learning-based control.
title: Lei Zheng

content: Tharusha Fonseka is a PhD student in the Safe and Control Learning Lab (SCLABLE) at the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, National University of Singapore. His research interests include safe learning control and Koopman operator methods.
summary: Tharusha Fonseka is a PhD student in the Safe and Control Learning Lab (SCLABLE) at the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, National University of Singapore. His research interests include safe learning control and Koopman operator methods.
title: Tharusha Fonseka

content: Zhihua Wu is a visiting PhD student in the Safe and Control Learning Lab (SCLABLE) at the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, National University of Singapore. He is currently a PhD student at Shanghai University. His research interests include smart grid and network control.
summary: Zhihua Wu is a visiting PhD student in the Safe and Control Learning Lab (SCLABLE) at the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, National University of Singapore. He is currently a PhD student at Shanghai University. His research interests include smart grid and network control.
title: Zhihua Wu

content: Hailong Wen is a PhD student in the Safe and Control Learning Lab (SCLABLE) at the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, National University of Singapore. His research interests include safe learning control and event-triggered learning.
summary: Hailong Wen is a PhD student in the Safe and Control Learning Lab (SCLABLE) at the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, National University of Singapore. His research interests include safe learning control and event-triggered learning.
title: Hailong Wen

content:
We warmly welcome Prof. Yuhan Liu, Associate Professor from Harbin Institute of Technology (HIT), for a research visit to SCLABLE in July 2026.
Armin Lederer, Lei Zheng, Zhihua Wu, and Tharusha Fonseka look forward to meeting with Prof. Liu to discuss learning-based control, robotics, and safe autonomy.
Learn more about Prof. Liu at https://homepage.hit.edu.cn/liuyuhan?lang=en.
summary:

title: Welcome Prof. Yuhan Liu (HIT) for a research visit
